name: Simple Chocolate Coating
recipeInstructions:
Do not use butter, margarine, or oil.
Cover a tray with wax paper. In a medium heat-proof bowl, place chocolate chips and shortening. In a separate large heat-proof bowl, put very warm water (100 to 110 F.) to a 1-inch depth. Carefully place the bowl with chocolate into the bowl with water; the water should come halfway up the side of the chocolate bowl. With a dry rubber scraper, stir the chocolate and shortening constantly until melted and smooth. Do not allow water to get into the bowl with chocolate.
If water cools, replace it with very warm water as directed above. Remove the chocolate bowl from inside the water bowl. Using a fork, dip chilled centers into the chocolate mixture one at a time, gently tapping the fork on the edge of the bowl to remove excess chocolate.
Invert coated center onto the prepared tray; swirl a small amount of melted chocolate over the top to cover any fork marks; let stand until firm. Store candies loosely covered in a cool, dry place. Coating for 3 to 4 dozen centers.
If water or moisture gets into the chocolate, it can seize and tighten. As an emergency measure, stir in additional shortening, half a teaspoonful at a time, just until the chocolate is fluid again.
If the chocolate mixture cools below 84 F, return the bowl with chocolate to the bowl with warm water. Chocolate is best for coating between 84 and 88 F.
Chocolate Candy in Molds: Melt chocolate with shortening as directed above; remove the bowl from inside the water bowl. Spoon the chocolate mixture into plastic candy molds; let stand until firm. Invert molds over the prepared tray; tap candy out of molds onto the tray. Store as directed above. About 4 dozen 1x1-1/2 inch candies.
